title: Neo bets $30M that workplace software should be rebuilt around AI
friendlyTitle: Neo wants to build an AI-first office suite
summary: TechCrunch reported that Indian entrepreneur Bhavin Turakhia is investing $30 million of his own money into Neo, an AI-native enterprise work platform combining projects, documents, files, and model-agnostic AI. The company argues that AI should be embedded into the work suite rather than bolted onto older office tools.
friendlySummary: Neo is a new work platform that aims to combine documents, projects, files, and AI from the start.
whyItMatters: The office-suite market is becoming a test of whether AI-native products can beat incumbents that add assistants to legacy workflows. For users, the question is whether AI feels like a separate chatbot or an active participant in everyday documents and projects.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This could change how teams write, plan, store files, and use AI during normal work.
